personality_voice.book_baseline: Sirius Black is a major character in the Harry Potter series, appearing from book 3 onward. His book characterization includes fierce loyalty, reckless bravery, deep trauma from Azkaban, paternal warmth toward Harry, bitterness and impulsiveness, dark humor, and a complex moral identity as a wrongly imprisoned man.
personality_voice.film_portrayal: Sirius Black does not appear in any scene in the provided film corpus. The corpus covers material from Chamber of Secrets (film 2), in which Sirius Black has no role.
personality_voice.difference: There is no film material for Sirius Black in this corpus whatsoever. No dialogue, no actions, no reactions from other characters referencing him.
personality_voice.penalty_logic: It is impossible to evaluate personality or voice without any corpus evidence. A score of 0 is assigned not as a faithfulness penalty but because there is no evaluable material.

narrative_role_agency.book_baseline: Sirius Black is a central figure from Prisoner of Azkaban onward: escaped convict, godfather to Harry, member of the Order of the Phoenix, and a key emotional and narrative anchor for Harry's identity and sense of family.
narrative_role_agency.film_portrayal: Sirius Black is entirely absent from the provided film corpus.
narrative_role_agency.difference: No film scenes featuring Sirius Black are present in the corpus.
narrative_role_agency.penalty_logic: No evaluable material. Score of 0 reflects absence of evidence, not a faithfulness judgment.

motivations_internal_conflict.book_baseline: Sirius is driven by love for Harry, guilt over James and Lily's deaths, hatred of Pettigrew, and the psychological damage of twelve years in Azkaban. His internal conflict includes recklessness born of trauma and a tension between his protective instincts and his desire for revenge.
motivations_internal_conflict.film_portrayal: Sirius Black does not appear in the provided film corpus.
motivations_internal_conflict.penalty_logic: Score of 0 reflects total absence of evaluable corpus evidence.

character_arc.book_baseline: Sirius's arc spans books 3 through 5: from feared fugitive to revealed innocent, to Harry's godfather and surrogate parent, to his death in the Department of Mysteries. It is a tragic arc of a man who never fully escapes the shadow of Azkaban.
character_arc.film_portrayal: Sirius Black does not appear in the provided film corpus.
character_arc.penalty_logic: Score of 0 reflects total absence of evaluable corpus evidence.

key_relationships.book_baseline: Sirius's most important relationships are with Harry (godfather/surrogate son), Lupin (old friend and fellow Marauder), James Potter (deceased best friend whose memory shapes Sirius's identity), and Pettigrew (betrayer and object of obsession).
key_relationships.film_portrayal: Sirius Black does not appear in the provided film corpus.
key_relationships.penalty_logic: Score of 0 reflects total absence of evaluable corpus evidence.

complexity_nuance_lost_material.book_baseline: Sirius is one of the most complex characters in the series: simultaneously heroic and reckless, loving and self-destructive, a victim who can also be cruel (e.g., to Kreacher, to Snape). His complexity is inseparable from his trauma.
complexity_nuance_lost_material.film_portrayal: Sirius Black does not appear in the provided film corpus.
complexity_nuance_lost_material.penalty_logic: Score of 0 reflects total absence of evaluable corpus evidence.

key_observations: The provided corpus contains no scenes featuring Sirius Black in either the book or film sections. The book corpus consists entirely of scenes from Chamber of Secrets (Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone/Chamber of Secrets material), in which Sirius Black does not appear. The film corpus is likewise drawn entirely from Chamber of Secrets, where Sirius has no role. All dimension scores are 0 and all confidence levels are Low solely because there is no evaluable material in this corpus. This evaluation cannot produce a meaningful faithfulness score for Sirius Black. A valid evaluation would require a corpus drawn from Prisoner of Azkaban onward.
